name: Bymaster Bankruptcy Law Offices
description_short: Indianapolis-based bankruptcy law office specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings with $0 down payment options and both in-person and online service.
description_long:
Bymaster Bankruptcy Law Offices was founded in 2008 by attorney John Forest Bymaster and his sister Anna Carothers (now Bymaster) in response to the economic crisis. The firm was deliberately structured around three core principles: accessibility, affordability, and a stress-free, hospitable client environment. The original office is located northwest of Indianapolis but has expanded to serve all of Indiana through virtual appointments.

The firm specializes exclusively in consumer bankruptcy relief, primarily Chapter 7 (liquidation) and Chapter 13 (reorganization/debt consolidation) filings. Chapter 7 cases eliminate unsecured debts like credit cards, medical bills, and personal loans within approximately 60 days. Chapter 13 cases consolidate debts into a single monthly payment over 3-5 years and can halt foreclosures or aggressive collection activities. The firm advertises $0 upfront payment options and describes itself as offering "super affordable" legal fees on the low end of the market spectrum.

Bymaster distinguishes itself through several operational choices: they emphasize in-person relationship-building while maintaining modern virtual capabilities, they offer free initial consultations with no obligation, they provide free supplementary resources (financial plans, credit repair plans, bankruptcy e-books), they claim to have filed thousands of cases, and they advertise a simplified, step-by-step process with brief trustee meetings (10 minutes or less). The firm also offers online document upload, online bankruptcy filing, and online course completion for mandatory bankruptcy education.

The firm appears legitimate and established (16+ years in operation), with founder credentials and client testimonials visible on the website. However, the website contains marketing language typical of consumer bankruptcy practices ("thousands" of cases, emphasis on affordability and friendliness) and lacks independent verification, detailed attorney credentials, disciplinary history, or specific client outcome data. The promise of $0 down likely depends on financial qualification and payment plan arrangements.
pros: $0 down payment options available for eligible clients
Free initial consultation with no obligation
Both in-person and online appointment availability across all of Indiana
Free supplementary resources including financial plans, credit repair plans, and bankruptcy e-books
Simplified 3-4 step process with transparent timelines (60 days for Chapter 7 discharge, 10-minute trustee meetings)
Emphasis on approachable, hospitable client experience in a stress-free setting
Established firm (founded 2008) with thousands of filed cases according to website
cons: Website contains marketing claims ("thousands of cases," "Indiana's Largest Bankruptcy Website") without independent verification or third-party ratings visible
No published attorney credentials, bar standing, or disciplinary history information on the website
Limited transparency on actual fee structures—'$0 down' and 'affordable' are vague without concrete pricing
No specific case outcome data, success rates, or discharge statistics provided
Reliance on single founder/attorney (John Bymaster) with no information about other attorney staff capabilities
best_for: Indiana residents with unsecured debt seeking Chapter 7 liquidation with minimal income
Homeowners facing foreclosure who need Chapter 13 reorganization to restructure mortgage payments
Consumers prioritizing in-person relationships and communication with their bankruptcy attorney
Low-income filers unable to afford upfront legal fees and needing payment plan options
diagnosis: Bymaster Bankruptcy Law Offices is best for Indiana consumers seeking affordable, personalized Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy relief who value in-person attorney relationships and have limited upfront capital. The main caveat is that the firm's marketing claims lack independent verification, actual fee structures are not transparently published, and there is no visible third-party rating or disciplinary history data available to assess legitimacy or performance.
services: Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing and debt reorganization
Mandatory bankruptcy education course completion (1st and 2nd course)
Client payment processing and account management
